summary: To determine criminal responsibility based on unsoundness of mind, one must prove an individual was unable to understand their actions were wrongful or illegal. This requires comprehensive psychiatric evaluation aligning closely with standards used in forensic psychology and psychiatry, as supported by various medical guidelines emphasizing accurate diagnosis through reliable methods akin to those employed in judicial settings. Key focus lies on confirming cognitive incapacity concerning moral judgment during the offense according to the McNaughten rule.
